title: Accept exact Desktop runtime artifacts in DAX preflight
description: Live Desktop proof exposed two native-preflight mismatches after a successful refresh: Desktop creates artifact-local .pbi/cache.abf and .pbi/localSettings.json while the exact PBIP is open, and Desktop 2.155 serializes customTheme.reportVersionAtImport as a version object. model dax execute must validate the exact open project without rejecting only those expected runtime files, while normal offline validate/package/handoff continue to reject them. Native theme validation must accept the canonical Desktop object shape as well as the existing non-empty legacy string, with fail-closed malformed cases. Add neutral regressions, rerun full Windows/WSL gates, then complete the live DAX proof.
close_reason: Live Desktop refresh/save/DAX proof passed; root artifact .pbi is accepted only by live DAX preflight, schema 2/3 theme shapes are exact, offline paths remain strict, and Windows/WSL full gates plus Clippy are green.

title: Render Power Query resource paths without Windows verbatim prefix
description: Live Desktop refresh of the exact consumer workflow output proved that File.Contents received a canonical Windows verbatim path beginning \\\\?\\C:\\; Power Query rejects it as not a valid absolute path, blocking both replaced base queries and dependents. Preserve canonical identity and hash checks internally, but render a normal drive/UNC path into M. Add Windows regression plus exact workflow/Desktop reproof; no generated-report hand patch.
close_reason: Fixed at the M serialization boundary: canonical security paths remain internal while Power Query receives normal drive/UNC spelling. Windows/WSL regression tests and full Windows suite pass.

title: Fix official PBIR alt-text and theme conformance
description:
## Why
Microsoft report-authoring CLI 0.1.4 rejects two shapes generated by existing powerbi-cli commands: altText is written under visual.objects.general although official metadata marks it as shared visualContainerObjects.general, and built-in theme JSON uses a display name that differs from the registered filename referenced by report.json.

## Scope
Fix the existing public CLI only, with neutral fixtures: src/report_visual_formatting_text.rs, src/pbir_visual_factory.rs, src/lint.rs, src/report_themes.rs, contract/help/skill wording, and affected tests/goldens. Do not depend on or mention any downstream repository in code or fixtures.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Use report visuals formatting set-text --alt-text or add a generated visual, then inspect visual.json: altText is under visual.objects.general.
2. Run npx --yes @microsoft/powerbi-report-authoring-cli@0.1.4 formatting describe-property card general altText; it reports _source=visualContainerObjects.
3. Apply the built-in risk-dashboard theme and run the exact official validate --no-schema command; it reports PBIR_FORMATTING_PROP_UNKNOWN and PBIR_THEME_FILE_NAME_MISMATCH.
## Implementation
- Read/write/clear altText at /visual/visualContainerObjects/general/0/properties/altText.
- Migrate an existing legacy powerbi-cli altText from visual.objects.general during an explicit set/clear mutation without disturbing unrelated general properties.
- Generate new visuals with the canonical shared VCO shape.
- Lint/readback prefer canonical shape and report legacy placement with an actionable code.
- Generate built-in theme JSON name equal to the registered resource filename; keep the human-readable preset display name in catalog metadata only.
- Preserve dry-run/out-dir/in-place guards and literal-text consent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Official formatting describe-property reports _source=visualContainerObjects and neutral CLI tests assert the same path.
- Official validate --no-schema reports no altText or theme-name diagnostic on a neutral generated fixture.
- Existing title behavior and unrelated visual.objects/general properties remain byte/semantically intact.
- Legacy misplaced altText can be corrected through the public set-text command.
- Existing command paths, flags, output envelopes, and preset IDs remain compatible; changed JSON pointers are documented.
- All legacy and new tests pass.

## Verification
Run focused report/theme/lint tests, cargo test --locked, native strict validation, exact @microsoft/powerbi-report-authoring-cli@0.1.4 validation on neutral fixtures, git diff --check, and UBS on changed files.

## Safety
Never mutate without the existing dry-run/in-place/out-dir contract. Do not bulk-rewrite arbitrary visual JSON or change unrelated formatting. No network is used by normal CLI commands.
close_reason: Canonical PBIR alt-text/theme generation, legacy migration, lint/readback, neutral goldens, and exact Microsoft 0.1.4 conformance are proven.

title: Microsoft Power BI agentic integration
description:
## Why
powerbi-cli needs one excellent public Power BI authoring control plane: deterministic native PBIP/PBIR work plus Microsoft's local semantic engine, official report validator, and Desktop Bridge.

## Scope
Ship the reviewed integration as a single coherent CLI. Rust owns project/report/source workflow and verification. Exact pinned Microsoft tools are locally installed sidecars for the capabilities they uniquely provide. The downstream report repository remains a consumer.

## Success Criteria
- Every current integration child bead is closed against machine-readable evidence and commit references.
- One obvious path exists for install/status, source-profile plan/run/verify, official validation, owned Desktop proof, and release.
- Normal native commands never download or contact npm; Microsoft tools install only through the explicit integration command.
- The public repository remains neutral, credential-free, and independently testable.
- Obsolete compatibility fields, dual paths, and unproved transaction semantics are removed instead of preserved.

## Verification
Run locked Rust tests, exact-package conformance, WSL/Linux-supported lanes, fresh-eyes reviews, simplification metrics, multi-pass bug hunting, UBS, Beads compliance, and consumer proof.

## Safety
Offline model writes occur only in disposable workflow-owned copies. Source inputs remain byte-identical. Vendor exports are quarantined and structurally verified. Desktop cleanup is limited to an exactly matched workflow-owned PID. No credentials, raw data rows, or private consumer artifacts are persisted.
close_reason: Outcome shipped in aac26a3 on origin/main: deterministic Rust control plane plus exact local Microsoft Modeling MCP, report validator and Desktop Bridge; one staged workflow, honest contracts, no compatibility/fallback path, independently reviewed and release-verified.

title: P0 Reviewed baseline and repository boundary
description:
## Why
Implementation needs one reviewed, reproducible public baseline and a hard repository boundary before optional Microsoft tooling can change behavior.

## Scope
Own docs/microsoft-powerbi-agentic-integration-plan.md, baseline test evidence, exact upstream pin evidence, and the neutral public/downstream separation. Do not implement runtime integration in this bead.

## Implementation
- Preserve the four-pass review log and resolve every P0/P1 finding.
- Record the clean baseline commit and cargo test result.
- Keep only public package pins, platform facts, and neutral acceptance in the plan.
- Use repository evidence, Beads graph checks, and independent subagent review as the review gate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Plan status and review log are internally consistent and implementation-ready.
- cargo test --locked passes at the recorded baseline.
- Public files contain no downstream repository name, domain formulas, paths, report counts, connector details, or screenshots.
- Public CLI release is explicitly independent of consumer repositories.
- Concrete independent-review findings are either applied or dispositioned with evidence.

## Verification
Record cargo test --locked, git diff --check, neutral-boundary rg checks, br lint/dep cycles/graph, bv robot output, and independent-review results in bead comments or evidence.

## Safety
Read-only discovery and documentation only. Do not weaken existing handoff/home safety and do not copy private evidence into this repository.
close_reason: Plan and Beads are implementation-ready; four reviews and graph checks complete; user explicitly waived the unavailable Fable review.

title: Reject PBIP artifact references that escape the selected project
description:
## Problem
PBIP report.path and PBIR semantic-model byPath values accept parent traversal without proving the resolved artifact remains inside the selected PBIP project. This undermines exact artifact-closure validation and workflow staging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Report references cannot escape the PBIP project root lexically or through links.
- Normal Report -> ../SemanticModel sibling references remain supported inside the project.
- Missing in-project artifacts retain structured validation behavior.
- Neutral tests cover traversal, sibling success, and linked escape where supported.
close_reason: Fixed selected-project containment for PBIP report and PBIR semantic-model references, including existing-link escape detection. Focused smoke regressions pass for lexical escape and valid root-relative sibling artifacts.

title: Accept canonical tab-indented TMDL child objects
description: The exact Microsoft Modeling MCP neutral fixture emits tab-indented table children. Existing TMDL parsing recognized partition/measure child starts only with four literal spaces, so exact staged partition lookup failed. Generalize indentation-aware child detection and block ranges for tabs and spaces, preserve trailing metadata boundaries, add neutral regressions, and rerun disposable staged-model tests.
notes: Fixed indentation-aware table-child parsing for canonical tabs and spaces using exact TMDL indent width; generalized source/expression indent stripping and insertion-boundary detection; preserved source range termination before trailing annotation/extendedProperty. Evidence: cargo test --locked tmdl::tests --no-fail-fast => 2 passed; focused staged-model tests => 5 passed; exact beta.11 disposable workflow => passed.
close_reason: Canonical tab-indented Microsoft fixture parses correctly; complete M source excludes trailing metadata; disposable staged-model regressions pass.

title: Modeling MCP offline connections require disposable-session isolation
description: The pinned Modeling MCP rejects its live-only coordination primitive on offline ConnectFolder models. The public design therefore has one disposable offline session: closed Update, exact readback, fresh quarantined export, process termination/reap/join, source and stage byte-identity proof, then native exact-range materialization with a complete expected-tree hash. The public result contains only the verified operation, hashes, export proof, and cleanup evidence. Exact-package and failure-injection tests are required; no alternate mode or compatibility contract is retained.
close_reason: Closed in aac26a3: one disposable offline session path replaces the unsupported live coordination primitive; exact MCP regression and complete cleanup/evidence proof green.

title: P1 Exact Microsoft toolchain
description:
## Why
Normal commands need reproducible access to exact Microsoft tools without npx drift, surprise downloads, or inherited-secret exposure.

## Scope
Commit the complete npm graph and platform integrities; implement exact cache status/install, doctor integration, contracts, and conformance tests.

## Implementation
- Resolve only pinned modeling-mcp, report-authoring, and desktop-bridge components.
- Install atomically into an immutable user cache only through integrations install --allow-network.
- Shallow status launches no child; deep status runs bounded exact checks against the installed cache.
- Launch every child with a minimal allowlisted environment and bounded/redacted output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Normal commands never contact npm or install tooling.
- Missing --allow-network fails before registry/proxy access.
- Failed install leaves the previous active cache byte-identical.
- Supported artifacts match exact versions and integrities; unsupported platforms fail closed.
- JSON reports canonical identities, consent/mutation metadata, verified cache state, child count, and changes.
- doctor preserves its existing contract and appends one child-free shallow integration summary.

## Verification
Unit-test lock/schema/platform/environment/output bounds and atomic activation. Run the real exact install/status graph on supported CI hosts, plus doctor/catalog/contract and full locked tests.
close_reason: Independent audit findings resolved with focused unit, integration, CI, and real exact-package evidence.

title: P2 Bounded Modeling MCP session
description:
## Why
The Modeling MCP is a long-lived stateful JSON-RPC process. The client must bound frames, cancellation, stderr, and the complete child lifetime while failing closed on protocol drift.

## Scope
Implement one synchronous bounded lifecycle in src/mcp.rs with standard worker threads, bounded channels, an independent process monitor, protocol fakes, and a real pinned handshake.

## Implementation
- Launch the verified local server with a minimal environment and stdio transport.
- Implement initialize, initialized, tools/list, request correlation, bounded framing, stderr tail/hash, and clean shutdown.
- Verify protocol/server version and normalized tools-list hash.
- Expose only the typed operations used by the disposable workflow.
- Track captured descendant PID/start identities, terminate them on graceful or forced cleanup, reap the root, and join every pump.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The real pinned server initialize/tools-list succeeds.
- Wrong version/hash, malformed or oversized frames, saturated queues, unknown IDs/tools, unsupported elicitation, and response-shape drift fail closed.
- Timeout/cancellation and monitor-start failure cannot leak or deadlock the owned process tree.
- No raw arbitrary MCP-call command or alternate runtime path exists.

## Verification
Deterministic fake tests cover fragmentation, saturation, stderr flood, timeout, cancellation, process descendants, spawn ownership, and protocol drift. Exact-package CI runs the handshake and disposable model workflow. Locked Windows and WSL/Linux tests pass.
notes: {"implementation":{"runtime":"std worker threads, bounded sync channels, independent monitor","bounds":"512KiB frame,4MiB response,32KiB stderr tail,30s call,120s session,3s cleanup","policy":"typed operations only; no raw public MCP call"},"verification":{"focused":"27 passed, 1 exact ignored","exactBeta11":"1 passed in 4.53s","strictClippy":"pass","windows":"pass","wslLinux":"pass"}}
close_reason: Closed in aac26a3: bounded typed MCP lifecycle, exact beta.11 handshake/workflow, process-tree cleanup, fake protocol failures, full Windows and isolated WSL suites green.

title: P3 Safe disposable staged model isolation and export
description:
## Why
Microsoft Modeling MCP can mutate a connected model and has a documented unsafe TMDL export shape. powerbi-cli must obtain semantic-engine readback without allowing the vendor process to touch the source project.

## Scope
Implement one disposable offline model-change path in src/mcp.rs and src/workflow.rs: exact folder connection, closed partition Update, exact readback, fresh quarantined export, process cleanup, and native exact-range materialization.

## Implementation
- Capture complete source and staged-definition tree hashes before starting the child.
- Inspect and credential-scan every complete M source expression before any process/output creation.
- Require exactly one canonical ConnectFolder identity.
- Send only typed partition replacements with closed request fields.
- Require exact partition readback for every changed table/partition.
- Export only into a pre-created empty workflow-owned wrapper/definition directory.
- Validate bounded TMDL shape; reject root files, unexpected paths, links/reparse points, and path escape.
- Terminate/reap/join the MCP before touching staged files.
- Reprove source and connected stage identity, then replace only parsed partition source ranges.
- Require the complete stage tree to match the precomputed expected-after hash.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Source is byte-identical on success and every injected failure.
- Missing write permission, credential-like input, schema drift, duplicate connection, bad readback, unsafe export, timeout, or cleanup failure stops before native materialization.
- The known unsafe source-root/export shapes are never passed to the server.
- Exact beta.11 succeeds against a disposable neutral TMDL fixture and its observed Update `{}` response is parsed narrowly.
- Failure receipts contain only bounded sanitized metadata; no M payload, credential, or raw vendor transcript persists.
- No in-place source write, generic raw MCP operation, dual path, or compatibility layer exists.

## Verification
Run fake failure injection, exact response-shape regressions, multi-file exact-tree tests, tab-indented TMDL regressions, the pinned beta.11 end-to-end proof, and source/stage/export hash assertions.
close_reason: Closed in aac26a3: disposable offline model export/materialization proves source/stage identity, rejects unsafe shapes and drift, exact beta.11 plus failure-injection and WSL/Windows tests green.

title: P4 Official report validation backend
description:
## Why
Native PBIR validation is deterministic but does not represent Microsoft official consumed-surface rules. Users need an explicit official validator with separate provenance.

## Scope
Add validate --backend native|microsoft-report|all using the exact pinned report-authoring package, neutral fixtures, bounded diagnostics, and discrepancy records.

## Implementation
- native is the default deterministic validator.
- microsoft-report runs only the installed exact official validator against exactly one selected report.
- all requires native and official validation to complete and keeps their results separate.
- Missing tooling is dependency_unavailable; malformed, oversized, timed-out, or vendor-error responses fail with stable bounded errors.
- Preserve validator identity, codes, locations, and exact version provenance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exact official validation runs against neutral valid and invalid fixtures.
- Validator disagreement is fixed or documented without merging provenance.
- Ambiguous report selection and every child/protocol failure stay bounded and deterministic.
- No mirrored vendor CLI or arbitrary vendor argument pass-through is exposed.

## Verification
Run native, microsoft-report, and all on the real exact package in CI; run parser/error, catalog, help, conformance, and full locked tests.
close_reason: Acceptance criteria met with fake-boundary and real exact-package conformance; official/native disagreement and provenance documented.

title: P5 Honest Desktop Bridge integration
description:
## Why
The official Desktop Bridge can reload and capture individual report pages more precisely than primary-display automation, but it is preview tooling and cannot prove refresh, interactions, or semantic correctness.

## Scope
Extend the existing CLI/contracts with desktop bridge status/reload/screenshot-page/screenshot-all using bounded one-shot calls from src/microsoft.rs. The Bridge surface accepts an externally supplied exact PID; process opening and ownership remain outside this subgroup.

## Implementation
- Add the coherent desktop bridge subgroup without changing desktop open-check or desktop screenshot.
- Require exact canonical project path and PID; serialize operations per PID.
- Reload only a clean saved instance.
- Allow dirty-instance screenshots only as downgraded observations with hasUnsavedChanges=true and no on-disk-output claim.
- Guard PNG outputs outside the project and record page inventory, dimensions, hashes, tool/Desktop versions, PID, and path.
- Never claim, terminate, or make an externally supplied Desktop PID cleanup-eligible.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Missing, wrong, or ambiguous PID/path and dirty reload are refused.
- External PIDs are never cleanup-eligible or claimed as workflow-owned proof.
- Status page inventory equals capture inventory; every page yields a decodable nonzero-dimension PNG with hash/provenance.
- Failure/timeout is bounded and cannot delete prior evidence.
- Output explicitly disclaims refresh, save/reopen, interaction/drill, issue-dialog absence, and semantic correctness.
- WSL/Linux report honest unavailability while file/model commands remain usable.

## Verification
Unit-test identity, empty/wrong PID, dirty aggregate state, inventory mismatch, output guards, serialization, timeout, and external ownership. Run exact Bridge against a marked disposable synthetic project on opt-in self-hosted Windows; never operate on an unrelated or unsaved user instance.

## Safety
This subgroup never terminates Desktop. Screenshots stay outside PBIP and are path/hash references in receipts. Existing Desktop oracle semantics remain unchanged.
close_reason: Closed in aac26a3: external exact-PID Desktop Bridge status/reload/screenshots, bounded calls, FileId-owned outputs, four adversarial race tests and seven integration tests green.

title: P6 Deterministic staged workflow
description:
## Why
Users need one deterministic path from a reviewed source profile to a separately validated PBIP output, without manually sequencing copies, model edits, validation, Desktop proof, and packaging.

## Scope
Implement workflow plan/run/verify as one closed public contract. Reuse exact project resolution, disposable MCP model editing, validation, and output primitives. Do not add in-place publication, vendor command mirroring, whole-repository copies, generic workflow DSLs, or compatibility modes.

## Implementation
- Define one versioned source profile with named resources and typed partition source replacements.
- Resolve only the selected PBIP plus its referenced Report, SemanticModel, and registered resources.
- A replacement names the exact table/partition, expected-before source hash, complete M template, expected connector, and resource placeholders.
- plan resolves all inputs, rejects credentials/machine-specific tracked paths, computes deterministic fingerprints, and creates only a new plan file.
- run confirms the plan fingerprint, rechecks every input, builds a new out-dir closure, applies replacements through the disposable MCP proof, validates with native and official backends, and writes one receipt.
- verify recomputes hashes and validation/evidence claims without mutation.
- Failed outputs remain clearly marked for diagnosis; source inputs are never changed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Identical inputs produce the same plan fingerprint.
- Existing outputs, path escape, input drift, missing confirmation, schema drift, or credential-like content fail before model mutation.
- Source remains byte-identical on success and injected failure.
- A repository-root PBIP never copies sibling projects, data directories, caches, or .git.
- Complete transformed M expressions survive replacement; only exact partition source ranges change.
- The receipt records hashes, exact backend versions, validation counts, process cleanup, and evidence paths/hashes; it never stores M/DAX payloads, query rows, credentials, or images.
- verify detects output, receipt, or evidence tampering.

## Verification
Use neutral multi-project, transformed-M, drift, failure-injection, deterministic-fingerprint, tamper, exact-package, Windows, and WSL/Linux fixtures. Then apply the released workflow to the consumer report.

## Safety
All outputs are new and workflow-owned. Every vendor interaction is typed and bounded. No in-place source write or hidden alternate path exists.
close_reason: Closed in aac26a3: deterministic selected-closure source-profile plan/run/verify, capability-relative writes, fresh MCP evidence binding, tamper/drift/credential tests and exact sidecar E2E green.

title: P7 Public conformance, ergonomics, docs, and skill
description:
## Why
The integration is only maintainable if its public contracts, conformance evidence, CI, documentation, agent guidance, and learned ergonomics match the live implementation.

## Scope
Complete neutral tests under tests/testdata, Microsoft conformance COVERAGE/PROVENANCE/DISCREPANCIES, normal exact-package CI, README/CHANGELOG, contract/feature/help output, doctor, and skills/powerbi-cli/SKILL.md. Apply only corroborated CASS and implementation-friction improvements.

## Implementation
- Cover every reviewed behavior-matrix row with the smallest appropriate unit, contract, real-package, or opt-in Desktop test.
- Add a normal Node 20+ CI lane for exact Report validation and MCP initialize/tools-list; keep only Desktop E2E self-hosted/opt-in.
- Preserve deterministic existing report catalogs/features and global --json placement.
- Update capability metadata, suggested commands, docs, and repository skill from actual command output.
- Audit implementation friction; fix public CLI/doctor/skill issues with neutral reproductions and no speculative framework expansion.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All legacy and new tests pass on supported Windows/WSL/Linux paths.
- Every new next[] command has a matching capability path and cataloged flags.
- COVERAGE.md maps consumed official surface to tests; PROVENANCE records exact versions/integrities/environment; DISCREPANCIES has no unexplained entry.
- README, help, doctor, features, contracts, and skill agree with live behavior.
- CASS-derived changes are backed by current code or reproduction.
- No downstream-work names or artifacts enter public fixtures/docs.

## Verification
Run cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy/tests as configured, exact-package CI locally where possible, capability/help/schema golden checks, UBS, git diff --check, public-boundary searches, and the repository skill examples.

## Safety
Documentation must not overclaim official conformance or Desktop proof. Test fixtures are neutral and credential-free. Optional tooling cannot make deterministic offline output environment-dependent.
close_reason: Closed in aac26a3: neutral conformance fixtures, CI matrix, README/architecture/workflow docs, live contract/help/doctor/skill updates; boundary scan, fmt, Clippy, full tests and crate packaging green.

title: P8 Verify and release the public CLI
description:
## Why
A public integration is complete only after independent graph review, full verification, protected-state inspection, and a traceable push; downstream consumer success must not be used to excuse public failures.

## Scope
Release the public repository only. Review all diffs and Beads evidence, run final quality gates, flush issue state, create the requested commit(s), and push the current branch. Downstream work is committed in its own repository under its own epic.

## Implementation
- Confirm every child acceptance criterion and evidence reference.
- Run bv robot graph/priority checks and br lint/cycle checks; resolve open blockers.
- Run full legacy, behavior, real-package conformance, WSL/Linux, and applicable Windows lanes.
- Run UBS and triage every finding; inspect git diff and repository boundary.
- Update release notes/version only if the repository's existing release policy requires it.
- Flush Beads before Git operations, then commit and push reviewed public changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Public release passes without access to any downstream repository.
- No P0/P1 defect, unexplained validator discrepancy, open blocking bead, cycle, secret, credential, generated cache, or downstream artifact remains.
- Working tree contains only intentional public files and generated Beads state allowed by policy.
- Commit references and final test evidence are recorded; push succeeds.
- The downstream work repository remains a separate consumer and release stream.

## Verification
Record br lint --json, br dep cycles --json, br graph/bv robot outputs, cargo/full CI commands, UBS result, git diff --check/status, secret/boundary scans, commit IDs, and push result.

## Safety
Do not weaken tests to ship, force-reset user work, commit caches/credentials, or conflate private consumer evidence with public acceptance. Preserve current branch policy and make only reviewed non-destructive Git operations.
close_reason: Closed after aac26a3 pushed to origin/main: full Windows and isolated WSL suites, strict Clippy, four exact Microsoft tests, verified 89-file cargo package, RustSec 104-dependency scan, UBS triage, lint/cycles/diff/boundary checks and independent fresh-eyes review green.

title: Resolve root-relative PBIP paths and validate only selected artifact closure
description:
## Problem
A root-relative .pbip filename has an empty Path::parent(), producing an exit-70 walk failure. Native validation also walks the containing repository and can absorb nested unrelated PBIP artifacts.

## Acceptance criteria
- Root-relative .pbip input resolves as current directory for validate and model commands.
- Validation checks only the selected PBIP plus its referenced Report and SemanticModel artifact trees.
- Regression tests cover the filename input and nested unrelated artifacts.
- Full relevant test suites pass.
close_reason: Root-relative filename resolves to current directory; validation walks only selected PBIP plus referenced Report/SemanticModel trees and skips missing trees so failures remain structured. Regression test passes; both consumer variants validate strict from explicit PBIP paths with exactly 60 selected JSON files and Microsoft official validation 0/0.

title: Make process-tree tests identity-safe under parallel Linux runs
description: Full WSL gates intermittently failed two process-tree assertions even though each passed repeatedly in isolation. The tests checked immediate PID absence after group termination, which can misread PID reuse or brief reap latency under parallel process churn. Capture PID plus start time before cleanup and use a bounded identity-aware disappearance check; production containment semantics remain unchanged.
close_reason: Process-tree tests now capture PID plus start time and poll boundedly for disappearance. Reproduced failures are gone; repeated isolated runs and full parallel Windows/WSL suites pass.

title: Make hierarchy authoring produce usable drill controls
description: Make report drilldown set-hierarchy author Desktop-ready category hierarchies: support line, area, bar, column, and combo charts; mark the first projection active; emit explicit visual-header drill controls; refuse scatter hierarchy because the official schema caps Category at one projection; and accept normal Desktop-authored predicate-free column/measure filter placeholders during validation.
acceptance_criteria: Hierarchy mutation produces official-valid PBIR with the first level active and all drill controls enabled; combo chart regression passes; scatter fails before writing; Desktop field-well placeholders validate; full tests and clippy pass.
notes: Verified with neutral hierarchy regressions, full all-target tests, and strict clippy.
close_reason: Implemented hierarchy activation and drill controls, combo support, official-safe scatter refusal, Desktop placeholder validation, documentation, and regressions; full test and clippy gates pass.
